page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the ecs.hfr7.2xlarge in the average performance tier across most categories. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1576.69, while the multi-core score reaches 8306.57. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 465.34 GB/sec, which drops to 51.09 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, alongside a write speed of 38.89 GB/sec. In database-related workloads, the instance achieves 7.99 million operations per second on Redis SET benchmarks and a Passmark database operations score of 3169.42. For specialized tasks, it processes 135M parameter LLM inference at 535.72 tokens per second for prompts, but performance drops to a weak 6.39 tokens per second for text generation on larger 7B parameter models.

Given its dedicated Intel Xeon processor cores and high memory density, the ecs.hfr7.2xlarge offers a balanced cost-to-resource profile for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage means users must rely on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is highly suitable for in-memory databases like Redis, caching layers, mid-sized database operations, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring dedicated CPU resources and high memory capacity.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369HB physical cores running at 3.3 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, but contains no local storage or GPU hardware. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place this server in the average tier, highlighted by a Passmark CPU score of 9658.56 and a Redis SET throughput of 7.99 million operations per second. Lacking local storage, it relies on network storage options. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring dedicated compute resources.
